{"key_event":"Constitutional Revolution and restoration of the Majlis","reasoning":"Urban merchants, ulama, reformist networks, newspapers, anjomans, and armed constitutionalist forces demonstrated sustained capacity to organize, defeat royal reaction, and restore parliament, raising civil society into the moderate-to-strong range. The Qajar state gained only limited coercive and fiscal tools while losing autonomy to foreign powers, facing provincial/tribal resistance, and failing to monopolize force, so central state power remained weak and slightly lower than in the 1890s.","society_change":1.3,"society_power":6.5,"state_change":-0.2,"state_power":3.0}
